popularStory.headline: WELLS\WALLIS FAMILY HISTORY FOLLOWS:
popularStory.description: HUGH THOMAS WELLS SR. was born about 1590 of Colchester, Essex, England, to Robert Wells (1540-1617) and Margaret Boshebe (1544-1615.) He married Frances Belcher about 1611 of Bedfordshire, England. …

zh.lifeSummary: Grace Wells是John Wells的女兒，於1607年2月12日在英國貝德福爾提爾布盧可受洗。她於1631年5月5日與Thomas Norton在英國英格蘭貝德福爾雪登結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1669年5月13日之後在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦賽布盧可，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 哈文吉爾福特。

nameMeanings.name: Wells
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Wells next the Sea (Norfolk) or Wells (Somerset), both named with the plural of Old English wella ‘spring, stream’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a group of springs or streams.
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dupuis   ‘from the well’.
History: One of numerous early immigrants from England bearing this name was Thomas Welles, governor of colonial CT, who was in Hartford, CT, by 1636.

variants.name: Dupuis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Pierre, Armand, Cecile, Normand, Emile, Lucien, Marcel, Rosaire, Alphonse, Henri.
topographic name for someone ‘from the well’, from Old French puis ‘well’, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’. Compare Dupee , Dupuie , Dupuy 2, and Wells .

nameMeanings.name: Grace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the abstract noun (via Old French, from Latin gratia), this name occurs occasionally in the 15th century, and by the 1540s was among the most popular girls' names in some parishes. It has always been particularly popular in Scotland and northern England (borne, for example, by Grace Darling , the lighthouse keeper's daughter whose heroism in 1838 , saving sailors in a storm, caught the popular imagination). In more recent times it was famous as the name of the actress Grace Kelly ( 1928–82 ), who became Princess Grace of Monaco. In Ireland it is used as an Anglicized form of Gráinne .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.
New Haven County was created on 10 May 1666, and was named for an English port town called Newhaven, London, England

en.lifeSummary: Grace Wells was christened on 12 February 1607, in Tilbrook, Bedfordshire, England as the daughter of John Wells. She married Thomas Norton on 5 May 1631, in Shelton, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died after 13 May 1669, in Saybrook, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, and was buried in Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut, United States.

it.lifeSummary: Grace Wells è stato battezzato il 12 febbraio 1607 nel Tilbrook, Bedfordshire, England come figlia di John Wells. Lei sposato Thomas Norton il 5 maggio 1631, nel Shelton, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Grace Wellsは1607年2月12日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアティルブルークでJohn Wellsの娘として洗礼を受けた。1631年5月5日にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアシェルトンでThomas Nortonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1669年5月13日以後にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ロンドンサイブルークで死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ニュー ヘイベングイルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Grace Wells(은)는 1607년 2월 12일 영국 베드퍼드실르 틸브룩에서 John Wells의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1631년 5월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 셸턴에서 Thomas Norton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1669년 5월 13일 이후 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 지브룩에서 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 길퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Grace Wells foi batizada em 12 de fevereiro de 1607, em Tilbrook, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra filha de John Wells. Ela casou-se com Thomas Norton em 5 de maio de 1631, em Shelton, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu após 13 de maio de 1669, em Saybrook, New London, Conecticute, British Colonial America, e foi sepultada em Guilford, New Haven, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Grace Wells была крещена 12 февраля 1607 г. в местности тилбрук, бедфордшир, Англия. Её отца звали John Wells. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Norton 5 мая 1631 г. в местности шелтон, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла после 13 май 1669 г. в местности сеибрук, ню лондон, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика и была похоронена в местности гуилфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Grace Wells se bautizó el 12 de febrero de 1607, en Tilbrook, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra como hija de John Wells. Se casó con Thomas Norton el 5 de mayo de 1631, en Shelton, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió después de 13 de mayo de 1669, en Saybrook, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, y fue sepultada en Guilford, New Haven, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
